text: Shri Digambar Jain Mandir - Jambudweep, Hastinapur. A global center for spiritual learning and Jain cosmology, inspired by Ganinipramukh Shri Gyanmati Mataji.
The vision of Jambudweep was born in 1965 during the meditation of Ganinipramukh Shri Gyanmati Mataji. It represents the physical structure of the universe as described in ancient Jain scriptures.
Jambudweep: A Living Classroom of Jain Cosmology. The complex features a 101-foot high Sumeru Parvat, representing the golden mountain at the center of our world island.

हस्तिनापुर की प्राचीनता (Antiquity of Hastinapur): One of India's oldest holiest cities, birthplace of 3 Tirthankaras: Shantinath, Kunthunath, and Arahanath. Pt. Nehru noted it as a twin pillar of ancient India alongside Indraprastha.
The architectural measurements of every structure in Jambudweep are precisely derived from the Tiloyapannatti and other Karananuyoga texts, ensuring spiritual and mathematical accuracy.
Education for All: The mission of the temple includes spreading the message of Ahimsa (Non-Violence) and providing spiritual education to visitors from all over the world.
Sumeru Parvat: The 101-foot marvel at the heart of Jambudweep. Visitors can climb the structural representations of the celestial levels to understand the Jain concept of heaven and liberation.
The 7 Islands and 7 Oceans: The landscape surrounding Sumeru Parvat is meticulously designed to represent the vast geography of our world island as detailed in Jain cosmology.
Kamal Mandir (Lotus Temple): A centerpiece of serenity, surrounded by water, where devotees can meditate and connect with the divine in a peaceful environment.
2008 Diamond Jubilee: A historic celebration marking the 75th birth anniversary of Ganini Gyanmati Mataji. The event hosted thousands of devotees and global leaders in Hastinapur.
Terahdwip Jinalaya: Inaugurated in 2007, this massive complex features 458 temples and 2,127 idols, making it one of the most comprehensive religious structures in the world.
The complex also includes representations of the Nandishwar Dwip, the eighth island of the middle world, which is celebrated for its 52 eternal Jinalayas.
A glimpse into the intricate carvings and structural details that make Jambudweep a masterpiece of modern Jain craftsmanship.
Sarvatobhadra Palace - Ayodhya: Representations of sacred sites related to the Tirthankaras, bringing the history of Jainism to the devotees.
Peace and Tranquility: The gardens and water bodies around the complex are designed to provide a meditative atmosphere for all visitors.
A dedicated research center at the complex focuses on the study and translation of ancient Jain texts, ensuring the wisdom is preserved for future generations.
Spreading the message: The temple organizes regular conferences and seminars on non-violence and environmental protection, following the tenets of Jainism.
Global Recognition: Jambudweep has been visited by dignitaries from across the globe, all inspired by its message of universal peace.
The complex also features a library with thousands of rare manuscripts and books on Jain philosophy, history, and science.
Community Service: The trust running the temple also manages schools, hospitals, and social welfare programs in the surrounding rural areas.

The Digambar Jain Trilok Shodh Sansthan serves as a beacon of research and faith. Under the guidance of Ganini Gyanmati Mataji, it has transformed Hastinapur into a global hub for Jain cosmological study.
Biography of a Visionary: Born as 'Maina' on 22 October 1934 in Tikaitnagar, UP. She took Aryika Diksha in 1956 from Acharya Shri Veersagar Maharaj, dedicating her life to spiritual upliftment.
The Statue of Ahimsa: A monumental achievement of the Sansthan. This 108-foot idol of Lord Rishabhdeva at Mangi-Tungi holds the Guinness World Record for the tallest Jain idol in the world.
Academic Excellence: In 1995, Ganini Gyanmati Mataji was awarded an honorary D.Litt. degree by Dr. Ram Manohar Lohia Avadh University for her immense contribution to literature and philosophy.
Shatkhandagam Commentary: A monumental literary work. Mataji authored a simple Sanskrit commentary on the 2,000-year-old scripture, making ancient wisdom accessible to the modern world.
Global Recognition: Dignitaries including PM Atal Bihari Vajpayee, President Pranab Mukherjee, and various Chief Ministers have visited Hastinapur to witness the spiritual grandeur of Jambudweep.
Supporting the Mission: The Sansthan manages various social welfare programs, including schools and hospitals, spreading the message of compassion (Karuna) alongside spiritual knowledge.
Connect with Us: Visit www.jambudweep.org or www.encyclopediaofjainism.com to explore the depth of Jain philosophy. Contact us at jambudweeptirth@gmail.com for pilgrimage inquiries.
